description: Profile self-management for patient/clinic/professional, full attendant CRUD scoped to clinic, and the linkedScheduling configuration that controls whether consecutive appointments at the same clinic require a minimum gap.

context.dependencies: Sprints 02 (RBAC), 06 (Schema enrichment) and 08 (Availability) must be complete

prompts.name: patient-schema-and-module
prompts.description: Create the Patient profile collection separate from the User document
prompts.request:
Before making any changes, read the existing files. Follow all conventions defined in medicano_crew/docs/CONVENTIONS.md. Check medicano_crew/docs/specs/feature-registry.md to understand what already exists.

The Address schema and AddressDto already exist (Sprint 06) — import them, do NOT recreate.

Create apps/api/src/patients/schemas/patient.schema.ts:
- @Schema({ timestamps: true }) class Patient:
  - @Prop({ type: Types.ObjectId, ref: 'User', required: true, unique: true }) userId: Types.ObjectId;
  - @Prop({ type: String, required: true }) name: string;
  - @Prop({ type: Date, required: true }) dateOfBirth: Date;
  - @Prop({ type: String }) phone?: string;
  - @Prop({ type: AddressSchema }) address?: Address;
- export type PatientDocument = Patient & Document
- export const PatientSchema = SchemaFactory.createForClass(Patient)
- PatientSchema.index({ userId: 1 }, { unique: true })

Create apps/api/src/patients/dto/update-patient-profile.dto.ts:
- class UpdatePatientProfileDto:
  - @IsString @IsOptional name?: string
  - @IsDateString @IsOptional dateOfBirth?: string
  - @IsString @IsOptional phone?: string
  - @ValidateNested @Type(() => AddressDto) @IsOptional address?: AddressDto

Create apps/api/src/patients/patients.module.ts:
- @Module imports MongooseModule.forFeature([{ name: Patient.name, schema: PatientSchema }])
- exports MongooseModule (so other modules can use the Patient model)

prompts.name: extend-user-schema
prompts.description: Add displayName and isActive to User schema for attendants

prompts.name: extend-clinic-schema-linked-scheduling
prompts.description: Add linkedScheduling field to Clinic schema (default false per RN25)
prompts.request:
Before making any changes, read the existing file. Follow all conventions defined in medicano_crew/docs/CONVENTIONS.md.

Update apps/api/src/clinics/schemas/clinic.schema.ts:
- Add @Prop({ type: Boolean, default: false }) linkedScheduling: boolean;
- Do NOT touch other fields

Create apps/api/src/clinics/dto/update-linked-scheduling.dto.ts:
- class UpdateLinkedSchedulingDto:
  - @IsBoolean linkedScheduling: boolean

prompts.name: profile-module
prompts.description: Self-managed profile endpoints for patient, clinic and professional
prompts.request:
Before making any changes, read the existing files. Follow all conventions defined in medicano_crew/docs/CONVENTIONS.md.

The following already exist — import them:
- UpdatePatientProfileDto at apps/api/src/patients/dto/update-patient-profile.dto.ts (created in prompt 1)
- UpdateClinicDto at apps/api/src/clinics/dto/update-clinic.dto.ts
- UpdateProfessionalDto at apps/api/src/professionals/dto/update-professional.dto.ts
- ClinicsService, ProfessionalsService
- Patient model (created in prompt 1)
- Role enum, Roles decorator, JwtAuthGuard, RolesGuard, CurrentUser decorator

Create apps/api/src/profile/dto/update-clinic-profile.dto.ts:
- export class UpdateClinicProfileDto extends UpdateClinicDto {} — pure re-export for clarity in URL-less context

Create apps/api/src/profile/dto/update-professional-profile.dto.ts:
- export class UpdateProfessionalProfileDto extends UpdateProfessionalDto {}

Create apps/api/src/profile/profile.service.ts:
- @Injectable class ProfileService
- constructor injects:
  - @InjectModel(Patient.name) patientModel: Model<PatientDocument>
  - @InjectModel('User') userModel: Model<UserDocument>
  - clinicsService: ClinicsService
  - professionalsService: ProfessionalsService

- async getMyProfile(userId: string): Promise<{ user: any; profile: any }>
  - const user = await this.userModel.findById(userId).select('-passwordHash')
  - if (!user) throw NotFoundException
  - let profile = null
  - switch (user.role):
    - case PATIENT: profile = await this.patientModel.findOne({ userId })
    - case CLINIC: profile = await this.clinicModel.findOne({ userId }) — use clinicsService
    - case PROFESSIONAL: profile = await this.professionalsService.findByUserId(userId)
    - case ATTENDANT: profile is null (the user document already has displayName)
  - return { user, profile }

- async updatePatientProfile(userId: string, dto: UpdatePatientProfileDto): Promise<PatientDocument>
  - findOneAndUpdate({ userId }, dto, { new: true, upsert: true, setDefaultsOnInsert: true })
  - On upsert, the userId must be set automatically — use $setOnInsert: { userId } in the update

- async updateClinicProfile(userId: string, dto: UpdateClinicProfileDto): Promise<ClinicDocument>
  - Find clinic where userId matches; throw NotFoundException if not found
  - Apply update via clinicsService.update(clinic._id, dto)

- async updateProfessionalProfile(userId: string, dto: UpdateProfessionalProfileDto): Promise<ProfessionalDocument>
  - Same pattern via professionalsService

Note: ClinicsService and ProfessionalsService should expose a findByUserId helper if they don't already. If they don't, instruct: 'Add a public findByUserId(userId: string) method to ClinicsService and ProfessionalsService that returns the clinic/professional owned by that user, or throws NotFoundException.'

Create apps/api/src/profile/profile.controller.ts:
- @Controller('profile') @UseGuards(JwtAuthGuard, RolesGuard)
- @Get('me') @SkipRoles or no @Roles → all authenticated users; calls getMyProfile(@CurrentUser() userId)
- @Put('me/patient') @Roles(Role.PATIENT) → updatePatientProfile
- @Put('me/clinic') @Roles(Role.CLINIC) → updateClinicProfile
- @Put('me/professional') @Roles(Role.PROFESSIONAL) → updateProfessionalProfile

Create apps/api/src/profile/profile.module.ts:
- imports: PatientsModule, ClinicsModule, ProfessionalsModule, MongooseModule.forFeature([{ name: 'User', schema: UserSchema }])
- providers: [ProfileService]
- controllers: [ProfileController]

prompts.name: attendants-crud
prompts.description: Full CRUD for attendants, scoped to the clinic owned by the authenticated user
prompts.request:
Before making any changes, read the existing files. Follow all conventions defined in medicano_crew/docs/CONVENTIONS.md.

The following already exist — import them, do NOT recreate:
- User schema and UserDocument at apps/api/src/auth/schemas/user.schema.ts
- Role enum, JwtAuthGuard, RolesGuard, Roles decorator
- ClinicsService for clinic ownership validation
- bcrypt npm package — use require('bcrypt') with cost 12

Create apps/api/src/clinics/dto/create-attendant.dto.ts:
- @IsString @MinLength(3) @MaxLength(30) @Matches(/^[a-zA-Z0-9_-]+$/) username: string
- @IsString @MinLength(8) password: string
- @IsString @MinLength(2) @MaxLength(80) displayName: string

Create apps/api/src/clinics/dto/update-attendant.dto.ts:
- @IsString @IsOptional displayName?: string
- @IsString @MinLength(8) @IsOptional password?: string
- @IsBoolean @IsOptional isActive?: boolean

Create apps/api/src/clinics/services/attendants.service.ts:
- @Injectable class AttendantsService
- constructor:
  - @InjectModel('User') userModel: Model<UserDocument>
  - clinicsService: ClinicsService

- private async assertClinicOwnership(clinicId: string, currentUserId: string): Promise<void>
  - const clinic = await this.clinicsService.findById(clinicId)
  - if (clinic.userId.toString() !== currentUserId) throw ForbiddenException('You do not own this clinic')

- async createAttendant(clinicId: string, currentUserId: string, dto: CreateAttendantDto): Promise<UserDocument>
  - assertClinicOwnership
  - const passwordHash = await bcrypt.hash(dto.password, 12)
  - try { user = await userModel.create({ role: Role.ATTENDANT, clinicId, username: dto.username, passwordHash, displayName: dto.displayName, isActive: true }) }
  - catch err with code 11000 → throw ConflictException('Username already taken in this clinic')
  - return user with passwordHash stripped (toObject and delete)

- async listAttendants(clinicId: string, currentUserId: string): Promise<UserDocument[]>
  - assertClinicOwnership
  - return userModel.find({ role: Role.ATTENDANT, clinicId }).select('-passwordHash').sort({ displayName: 1 })

- async updateAttendant(clinicId: string, attendantId: string, currentUserId: string, dto: UpdateAttendantDto): Promise<UserDocument>
  - assertClinicOwnership
  - validate attendantId is ObjectId
  - const update: any = {}
  - if (dto.displayName !== undefined) update.displayName = dto.displayName
  - if (dto.isActive !== undefined) update.isActive = dto.isActive
  - if (dto.password) update.passwordHash = await bcrypt.hash(dto.password, 12)
  - const user = await userModel.findOneAndUpdate({ _id: attendantId, role: Role.ATTENDANT, clinicId }, update, { new: true }).select('-passwordHash')
  - if (!user) throw NotFoundException('Attendant not found in this clinic')
  - return user

- async removeAttendant(clinicId: string, attendantId: string, currentUserId: string): Promise<{ success: true }>
  - assertClinicOwnership
  - const result = await userModel.deleteOne({ _id: attendantId, role: Role.ATTENDANT, clinicId })
  - if (result.deletedCount === 0) throw NotFoundException
  - return { success: true }

Create apps/api/src/clinics/controllers/attendants.controller.ts:
- @Controller('clinics/:clinicId/attendants')
- @UseGuards(JwtAuthGuard, RolesGuard) @Roles(Role.CLINIC)
- @Post('/') createAttendant(@Param clinicId, @CurrentUser userId, @Body dto)
- @Get('/') listAttendants(@Param clinicId, @CurrentUser userId)
- @Put('/:attendantId') updateAttendant(@Param clinicId, @Param attendantId via ParseMongoIdPipe, @CurrentUser userId, @Body dto)
- @Delete('/:attendantId') @HttpCode(204) removeAttendant(@Param clinicId, @Param attendantId, @CurrentUser userId)

Update apps/api/src/clinics/clinics.module.ts:
- Add MongooseModule.forFeature([{ name: 'User', schema: UserSchema }]) to imports if not already present
- Add AttendantsService to providers
- Add AttendantsController to controllers
- Export ClinicsService (if not already) so ProfileModule can use it

prompts.name: linked-scheduling-conflict-logic
prompts.description: Update AppointmentsService.checkConflict to honor linkedScheduling flag
prompts.request:
Before making any changes, READ the existing file CAREFULLY — preserve all existing methods. Follow all conventions defined in medicano_crew/docs/CONVENTIONS.md.

The ClinicsService already exists. The Clinic schema now has a linkedScheduling field (added in prompt 3).

Update the existing private checkConflict method in AppointmentsService:

Current signature:
  private async checkConflict(professionalId: string, startAt: Date, endAt: Date, excludeId?: string): Promise<void>

New signature:
  private async checkConflict(professionalId: string, startAt: Date, endAt: Date, clinicId: string, excludeId?: string): Promise<void>

New logic:
- const clinic = await this.clinicsService.findById(clinicId)
- const linkedScheduling = clinic.linkedScheduling ?? false
- Build query window:
  - if linkedScheduling === true: STRICT overlap only — { startAt: { $lt: endAt }, endAt: { $gt: startAt } } — touching boundaries do NOT conflict
  - if linkedScheduling === false: include adjacency — { startAt: { $lte: endAt }, endAt: { $gte: startAt } } — touching boundaries DO conflict
- Add filter: status NOT IN [CANCELLED]
- Add filter: professionalId
- Add filter: clinicId === this clinic (linkedScheduling only matters for SAME clinic — different clinics still go through RN04 in createForPatient)
- if excludeId: _id NOT EQUAL to excludeId
- if found, throw ConflictException

IMPORTANT: All existing call sites of checkConflict must be updated to pass clinicId:
- in create() method: this.checkConflict(dto.professionalId, startAt, endAt, dto.clinicId)
- in update() method: pass the clinicId from the existing appointment doc
- in createForPatient() (Sprint 09): this.checkConflict(dto.professionalId, startAt, endAt, dto.clinicId)

Do NOT remove the cross-clinic 30-min check (RN04, Sprint 09) — it stays as a separate method (checkCrossClinicInterval).

prompts.name: profile-and-attendants-tests
prompts.description: Unit tests for ProfileService and AttendantsService
prompts.request:
Before making any changes, read the existing files. Follow all conventions defined in medicano_crew/docs/CONVENTIONS.md.

Create apps/api/src/profile/tests/profile.service.spec.ts:
- Mock userModel, patientModel, clinicsService, professionalsService

Tests:
1. 'getMyProfile — patient returns user + Patient document'
2. 'getMyProfile — clinic returns user + Clinic document'
3. 'getMyProfile — professional returns user + Professional document'
4. 'getMyProfile — attendant returns user with profile=null'
5. 'updatePatientProfile — upsert creates patient on first call' (mock findOneAndUpdate to confirm upsert: true)
6. 'updatePatientProfile — update modifies fields when patient exists'

Create apps/api/src/clinics/tests/attendants.service.spec.ts:
- Mock userModel and clinicsService
- Mock bcrypt.hash to return 'hashed-password'
- afterEach jest.clearAllMocks()

Tests:
1. 'createAttendant — success hashes password and creates user with role=ATTENDANT'
2. 'createAttendant — duplicate username throws ConflictException' (simulate Mongo error code 11000)
3. 'createAttendant — clinic owned by another user throws ForbiddenException'
4. 'listAttendants — returns only attendants of that clinic, no passwordHash, sorted by displayName'
5. 'listAttendants — wrong clinic owner throws ForbiddenException'
6. 'updateAttendant — wrong clinic throws NotFoundException' (mock findOneAndUpdate to return null)
7. 'updateAttendant — password rehashed when provided' (verify bcrypt.hash called)
8. 'updateAttendant — password NOT rehashed when not provided'
9. 'removeAttendant — success returns { success: true }'
10. 'removeAttendant — non-existent throws NotFoundException' (deletedCount 0)

prompts.name: linked-scheduling-tests
prompts.description: Add 3 conflict tests covering linkedScheduling=true and =false
prompts.request:
Before making any changes, READ the file and PRESERVE all existing tests. Follow all conventions defined in medicano_crew/docs/CONVENTIONS.md.

The AppointmentsService now injects ClinicsService — make sure it's mocked in the TestingModule. Mock clinicsService.findById to return { _id, linkedScheduling: <true|false>, ... } per test.

Add these tests inside the existing describe('AppointmentsService') block:

1. 'checkConflict — linkedScheduling=true allows adjacent same-clinic appointments'
   - Mock clinicsService.findById to return { linkedScheduling: true }
   - Existing appointment: 09:00-10:00 at clinic A, professional X
   - New appointment: 10:00-11:00 at clinic A, professional X
   - Expect create to succeed (no ConflictException)

2. 'checkConflict — linkedScheduling=false blocks adjacent same-clinic appointments'
   - Mock clinicsService.findById to return { linkedScheduling: false }
   - Same scenario as above
   - Expect ConflictException

3. 'checkConflict — linkedScheduling=true still blocks STRICT overlap'
   - Mock clinicsService.findById to return { linkedScheduling: true }
   - Existing appointment: 09:00-10:00
   - New appointment: 09:30-10:30 (overlapping)
   - Expect ConflictException

If existing tests break because checkConflict now requires clinicId, UPDATE them to pass a valid clinicId and mock clinicsService.findById to return a clinic with linkedScheduling=false (default behavior matching current tests).
